danger_signals.visual_form: On-board computer HUD feedback indicating proximity of enemy forces
Computer readout showing loss of limbs, actuators, or structural integrity
danger_signals.meaning: Mech structural compromise; reduced combat effectiveness

danger_signals.visual_form: Rising temperature gauge on mech's on-board computer
danger_signals.meaning: Weapon overheating; risk of ammunition explosion and fusion engine detonation
Reduced offensive capability; need to manage firepower

danger_signals.visual_form: Computer automatic shutdown attempt when heat exceeds safe levels
danger_signals.meaning: Critical thermal condition; loss of mech control imminent unless overridden

safety_signals.visual_form: On-board computer HUD indicating location of friendly units
safety_signals.meaning: Allied support available; reduced isolation risk

safety_signals.visual_form: Computer readout showing intact armor, weapons, and actuators
safety_signals.meaning: Mech operationally sound; full combat capability maintained

safety_signals.visual_form: Stable temperature gauge within safe operating range
safety_signals.meaning: Weapon systems stable; no risk of catastrophic failure

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Jade Falcon mech paint schemes and loadouts differ from Clan Wolf
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Missions assigned to Jade Falcon faction occur on specific canonical planets in the Inner Sphere campaign

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Wolf mech configurations visually distinct from Jade Falcon units
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Wolf-faction missions deploy on different planetary theaters; represents Warden ideology opposition to Crusader Jade Falcons

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Players must choose faction affiliation (Jade Falcon Crusader vs. Wolf Warden) at game start, establishing ideological split visually through mech designs and mission briefings
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Enemy AI behavior and mission objectives reflect Refusal War conflict; player faction determines which opponents are encountered and campaign progression

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Battlefields confined to specific disputed planets rather than broader Inner Sphere conquest; limited territorial scope reflected in mission map design
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Missions focus on territorial skirmishes and ideology vindication (Ulric Kerensky trial by combat) rather than systematic invasion advance

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Jade Falcon (Crusader) vs. Wolf (Warden) mech designs and unit compositions differ visually to reflect faction doctrine
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Campaign missions alternate between defending Warden philosophy or pursuing Crusader conquest goals based on player clan selection

world_state_to_visual.world_state: High-tech military society based on meritocracy ('might equals right')
world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Highly detailed mech customization systems visible in mech lab interface; weapon/armor/engine configurations reflect individual combat effectiveness
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Combat resolution through trial-by-combat mechanics; single powerful units (mechs) can determine mission outcome, not numerical superiority

environmental_storytelling.example: Battles occur on named planets from BattleTech franchise canon; each planetary environment implicitly communicates territorial dispute stakes and strategic importance without explicit exposition

Search-and-destroy, reconnaissance, and base strike missions communicate shifting military priorities and strategic situation; changing objectives signal campaign progression and faction-specific goals

Inter-mission mech lab access allows players to upgrade based on mission results and resource management, visually representing faction resource availability and technological capability over campaign timeline
Progression from single-mech control to squad commands communicates player advancement in military hierarchy and reflects increasing faction investment in player character

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Responsive to all control inputs; weapons fire, movement, torso/leg independent management available based on control mode selection
On-board computer attempts automatic emergency shutdown; player can override, but risk of ammunition explosion and limb loss increases
Loss of limbs or actuators reduces mobility and weapon hardpoint availability; visual feedback shows structural integrity degradation
Computer HUD displays proximity and faction affiliation; enemy unit type indicates encounter difficulty and required tactical response
On-board computer tracks location and status; presence reduces isolation and provides tactical coordination opportunity (squad commands in later missions)

notes: MechWarrior 2 communicates world-state primarily through faction selection and mission assignment rather than environmental visual transformation. The Refusal War ideological conflict manifests through binary clan choice (Jade Falcon Crusader vs. Wolf Warden) which determines campaign narrative and enemy faction. Danger is signaled through mech-centric HUD feedback (heat, damage, enemy proximity) rather than environmental hazards, reflecting the simulation's focus on piloting and thermal management. Political situation (post-Tukayyid, trial-by-combat) is conveyed through mission briefings and objective framing rather than dynamic world changes. Visual faction differentiation occurs through mech paint schemes and unit composition rather than environmental markers.
